disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Widespread thick red plaques with dense silvery scale on extensor sites, cracking/bleeding on movement, on a long-standing scaly-skin background, after a strep throat
trigger_question_examples: Are these thick red plaques with silvery scale on your elbows/knees that crack when you move, on your usual scaly-skin background?
Did it flare after a throat infection or stress?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, thick red plaques with silvery scale on my elbows/knees and back that crack and bleed when I move, and it flared after a strep throat.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Skin is NOT bright red all over (<90% BSA), no high fever/rigors, no feeling freezing/unable to keep warm (erythroderma/thermoregulation/sepsis screen)
trigger_question_examples: Is nearly your whole body bright red, or do you feel feverish/shivering or unable to keep warm?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's not my whole body — patches on the usual areas and trunk; no high fever or shivering and I can keep warm.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I just feel sore all over.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No thick yellow pus/spreading hot redness from the cracks (secondary-infection screen)
trigger_question_examples: Any thick yellow pus or spreading hot redness from the cracked skin?
Are the cracks oozing pus or getting hot and spreading?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No pus or spreading hot redness — just the cracks bleeding.
